name: Grandma's Cinnamon Rolls
recipeInstructions:
-------------
Filling:
1/4 cup Butter (melted)
3 tablespoons Sugar
3 tablespoons Brown Sugar
1/4 cup Nuts (chopped)
1 tablespoon Cinnamon (ground)
Glaze:
1 cup Powdered Sugar
1 1/2 tablespoons Butter (melted)
1/4 teaspoon Extract (maple)
2 tablespoons Coffee (hot)
Place ingredients into pan in the order listed.
Select manual or dough setting.
After the machine has completed the second rising, it will beep. In the Hitachi, take it out about 10 minutes before the beep as it has reached the top of the pan.
On a lightly floured surface, roll or pat to a 12" x 9-inch rectangle. Brush with melted butter. Combine sugars, nuts, and cinnamon; sprinkle over dough. Starting with the shorter side, roll up tightly. Pinch edge to seal. Cut into 12 slices. Place on greased 13" x 9-inch pan. Or cut 9 slices, discard the two end pieces, and place in a 9-inch round pan, with one in the middle and the rest arranged around the outside. (looks nice)
Cover; let rise in a warm place until almost double in volume (about 30 minutes).
Preheat oven to 375°F.
Bake 20-25 minutes.
To prepare glaze, combine sugar, melted butter, maple extract, and hot coffee. Blend until smooth. Drizzle over hot rolls.
